task.name: Parallel investigation orchestrator
task.objective: Read a goal's Investigation Config from goal.md, then SPLIT its investigators (step 2f): run static-analysis / pure-command checks INLINE in this window and spawn parallel read-only investigator-* workers ONLY for advanced validation (code-review, E2E/Chrome, integration, debugging) via windows-spawn-worker; collect any worker verdicts with a 10-minute timeout, aggregate the inline + worker + reused findings, write corrections/cycle-N.md on failure, and call goal-validation-done with the final pass/fail verdict.

input.arg: Path to goal.md file (e.g. .tmux-cli/goals/goal-008/goal.md). Passed by the taskvisor daemon when creating the validator window.

When present (force_full_revalidation=true), bypass the C10 incremental reuse gate and re-run EVERY investigator regardless of fingerprint. The daemon also forces a full sweep on the final cycle before overall pass.

output.primary: goal-validation-done MCP call with pass or fail verdict and findings array.
output.secondary: corrections/cycle-N.md in the goal directory (written only on fail verdict).

requirements.mcp: tmux-cli MCP server REQUIRED (for windows-spawn-worker, windows-message, windows-kill, goal-validation-done)

glossary.term: Name of this orchestrator's tmux window — ALWAYS the goal-namespaced "validator-{ns}" (e.g. validator-008). Created by the taskvisor daemon with UUID authorization for goal-validation-done. Resolved by reading GOAL_DIR/validator-window VERBATIM (the daemon wrote that marker byte-exact at dispatch), NOT by guessing the bare name "validator" and NOT by probing the active tmux window.
Name of an investigation worker window (e.g. "investigator-1", "investigator-2"). Each worker gets a unique N via windows-spawn-worker with prefix: "investigator-".
Goal identifier extracted from the goal.md file path (e.g. "goal-008" from .tmux-cli/goals/goal-008/goal.md).
Directory containing the goal (e.g. .tmux-cli/goals/goal-008/). Derived from the goal.md path.
Section in goal.md (## Investigation Config) defining 2-4 investigators with type, paths, commands, pass/fail criteria, and optional condition/retry fields.
A single check definition within the Investigation Config. Each active investigator becomes one investigator-* worker.
GOAL_DIR/corrections/cycle-N.md — written when any investigator fails. Contains per-investigator findings, failing commands, and expected state.
Overall result: "pass" only if ALL active investigators report pass. Any fail/timeout/error = "fail".

message-tags.note: Investigation workers use the standard [EXECUTE:...] tagged protocol. investigate.xml uses [SUPERVISOR:...] tags when communicating with workers. Semantics below are the source of truth.
message-tags.tag: Worker reports investigation complete. The .md at file= contains VERDICT, FINDINGS, and EVIDENCE sections.
Worker cannot proceed without a fact. Reason ≤ 200 chars inline.
Worker hit a terminal failure. Reason ≤ 200 chars inline.
Narrow a NEED_INPUT-blocked worker's scope instead of escalating.
Instruct worker to exit cleanly (used on timeout).
Protocol reminder to worker. Does not advance state.

llm.mandate: NEVER modify any project files — investigation is strictly read-only (only corrections/cycle-N.md and goal-validation-done are write actions)
NEVER use windows-send — use windows-message for all replies to workers
NEVER use sleep — idle is the trigger for incoming windows-message
NEVER kill your own VALIDATOR_WID — the daemon owns lifecycle
NEVER accept partial results — every spawned worker must reach a terminal state (completed/failed/timeout) before aggregation
NEVER use pushback — workers report pass or fail, no revision loop
ALWAYS use windows-spawn-worker with prefix: "investigator-" — never assemble spawn steps manually
ALWAYS call goal-validation-done exactly ONCE at the end with the aggregated verdict
Execute steps ONE BY ONE in EXACT ORDER

step.title: MCP precondition and project context
step.action: Call windows-list to verify tmux-cli MCP is available.

check.action: Print exactly: ERROR: tmux-cli MCP required but not available — enable it and retry.

step.action: Check if AGENTS.md exists in the project root. If it does, Read it and internalize project conventions.
If AGENTS.md does not exist, check for CLAUDE.md instead. Read whichever is found first.
If neither exists, proceed without project context.

Resolve VALIDATOR_WID self-identification (step 0b): extract GOAL_ID/GOAL_DIR from the $ARGUMENTS goal.md path (.tmux-cli/goals/<goal-id>/goal.md → GOAL_ID=<goal-id>, GOAL_DIR=.tmux-cli/goals/<goal-id>/), then read GOAL_DIR/validator-window and use its content VERBATIM (trim only trailing newline/whitespace) as VALIDATOR_WID. The daemon wrote this marker byte-exact (validatorWindow(goal.ID, MaxGoals)) BEFORE booting this window, so it is the goal-namespaced "validator-{ns}" — reading it verbatim binds VALIDATOR_WID to the exact spawned window without probing. Do NOT synthesize the name from the goal id and do NOT use the bare name "validator". FALLBACK ONLY if the marker is absent/unreadable: if a window named "validator" exists in windows-list use it, else use the active window.

step.title: Parse Investigation Config from goal.md
step.action: Read the file at $ARGUMENTS (the goal.md path).

check.condition: $ARGUMENTS is empty or file does not exist
check.action: Call goal-validation-done: goal_id="unknown", verdict="fail", findings=[{rule:"goal.md", status:"fail", detail:"goal.md path missing or file not found", correction:"provide valid goal.md path"}], next_action="fix goal.md path"

step.action: Extract GOAL_ID from the file path pattern: .tmux-cli/goals/<goal-id>/goal.md → GOAL_ID = <goal-id>.
Set GOAL_DIR = .tmux-cli/goals/<GOAL_ID>/
Locate the ## Investigation Config section in goal.md.

check.condition: section missing or has fewer than 2 investigator subsections
check.action: Call goal-validation-done: goal_id=GOAL_ID, verdict="fail", findings=[{rule:"Investigation Config", status:"fail", detail:"malformed or missing Investigation Config (need 2-4 investigators)", correction:"add ## Investigation Config with 2-4 ### Investigator subsections to goal.md"}], next_action="fix Investigation Config"

step.action: For each ### Investigator N subsection, extract:
step.field: name — the investigator label after the colon (e.g. "DDD compliance")
type — investigation type (code-review, static-analysis, e2e-test, integration-test, etc.)
paths — file/directory paths to examine (from "Paths:" or "Path:" line)
commands — commands to run (from "Command:" or "Commands:" lines)
condition — prerequisite condition (from "Condition:" line, e.g. "Node/npm available")
retry — retry config (from "Retry:" line, e.g. "3 total attempts")
check — what to verify (from "Check:" line, if present)
step.action: Evaluate conditions for each investigator:
step.rule: If an investigator has a Condition field, evaluate it against the project state (e.g. check if package.json exists for "Node/npm available", check if a file exists for path-based conditions). If the condition is false, mark the investigator as SKIPPED and do not spawn a worker for it.

C4-cont: if a Condition is a probe-style reachability/health check (e.g. "service X reachable", "health endpoint ready") rather than a static presence check, evaluate it through the <transient-retry-loop> defined in step 2b BEFORE concluding it is false — a transient infra hiccup must not silently skip a real investigator. Static presence checks (file/package.json exists) are deterministic and are NOT retried.
Log each skipped investigator with its name and the reason the condition was false.
step.action: After condition filtering, count active (non-skipped) investigators.

check.condition: zero active investigators (all were conditional and all skipped)
check.action: Log warning: "all investigators skipped — no checks to fail, defaulting to pass"

Call goal-validation-done: goal_id=GOAL_ID, verdict="pass", findings=[{rule:"Investigation Config", status:"pass", detail:"all investigators skipped (conditions not met) — pass by default"}], next_action=""

step.action: Also extract from goal.md: the goal description (first heading or first paragraph) and acceptance criteria (## Acceptance Criteria section) for worker context.

Also extract the OPTIONAL ## Lane section from goal.md into LANE: take the section's bare body, trimmed. LANE = "solo" ONLY on an exact match of `solo`; an absent section, empty body, `full`, or any malformed/unknown value → LANE = "full". The lane is decided upstream and is READ-ONLY here: goal.md's ## Lane is the ONLY lane source this orchestrator consults — never another file, and never its own evaluation of lane-gate criteria. LANE is recorded for audit only — the inline/spawn split (step 2f) keys off investigator TYPE and is lane-INDEPENDENT (B9b), so LANE never changes routing.
Build investigator tracking table:
step.format: | INV_WID | Investigator Name | Status | Report Path | Spawn Time | |---------|-------------------|--------|-------------|------------| | investigator-1 | DDD compliance | pending | null | null | | investigator-2 | Architecture | pending | null | null |

step.title: Resolve per-cycle research dir (CYCLE_DIR)
step.action: Read .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal (if present, trimmed → GOAL_ID_MARKER).
step.rule: GOAL MODE — taskvisor-current-goal exists and is non-empty: use the GOAL_ID derived from the goal.md path in step 2 (the marker only corroborates it). Resolve CYCLE_N per-goal first: (1) read GOAL_DIR/current-cycle — if present and it trims to a positive integer, that is CYCLE_N; (2) ONLY if the per-goal marker is absent, empty, or non-numeric, read the global .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-cycle (same trimmed-positive-integer check); (3) if both are missing/empty/non-numeric, default CYCLE_N=1. Set CYCLE_DIR = .tmux-cli/goals/<GOAL_ID>/research/cycle-<CYCLE_N>/ — a path BENEATH the already-existing goal-scoped research root; do NOT invent a parallel tree. NEVER derive CYCLE_N by scanning corrections/ or research/cycle-* directories — both drift from the daemon's authoritative cycle number.
STANDALONE MODE — taskvisor-current-goal absent or empty: there is no daemon-tracked cycle. Set CYCLE_DIR = .tmux-cli/research/{y-m-d-hh}/ (current timestamp, NO cycle layer) and leave CYCLE_N unset.
step.action: mkdir -p CYCLE_DIR (idempotent, defensive — the daemon pre-creates it in goal mode).

Read .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-worktree (if present, trimmed → WORKTREE_DIR). If the marker is absent or empty, leave WORKTREE_DIR unset — this is the MaxGoals=1 / no-worktree case and the investigator-* workers keep the session-default cwd (byte-identical to before).

step.title: Preflight precondition re-check (defensive fallback)
step.action: Locate the ## Preconditions section in goal.md (it sits between ## Validation Rules and ## Context). If the section is absent or has zero items, SKIP this step entirely — empty preconditions are all-pass and must never block.
Each item has the form: "- [kind] spec — remedy". For each item, in order, evaluate:
step.rule: kind=env: FAIL if the environment variable named <spec> is unset OR set to the empty string. failure_class="env-config", owner="ops". DETERMINISTIC — never retried (an unset env var will not appear by retrying).

kind=service: parse <spec> as host:port and probe a TCP connection within ~2 seconds. A connection failure is a TRANSIENT candidate — run it through the <transient-retry-loop> below BEFORE concluding. The precondition FAILs (failure_class="infra-flake", owner="ops") ONLY after the retry budget is exhausted; if ANY attempt connects, the precondition PASSES with no finding.
any other kind: FAIL. failure_class="spec-defect", owner="planner". DETERMINISTIC — never retried.

transient-retry-loop.rule: Read transient_retry_max_attempts and transient_retry_backoff_ms from .tmux-cli/setting.yaml (the taskvisor: block). If either key is absent (legacy setting.yaml), fall back INLINE to 3 / 500 respectively.

Attempt the probe up to transient_retry_max_attempts TOTAL times. N attempts means N-1 sleeps: between consecutive attempts sleep transient_retry_backoff_ms milliseconds. transient_retry_max_attempts=1 means a single try with NO retry loop body.

On each failure, classify the cause against <transient-detection-rules>. Matches ANY rule ⇒ TRANSIENT: sleep the backoff and retry while attempts remain. Matches NONE ⇒ NOT transient: stop immediately and fall through to the leaf-4 code-defect catch-all (C1) — do NOT retry, do NOT emit infra-flake.
If ANY attempt SUCCEEDS: stop, mark the check pass, emit NO finding, and do NOT escalate or invoke ClassifyVerdict.

If a TRANSIENT cause persists through the FINAL attempt (budget exhausted): emit ONE finding status="blocked", failure_class="infra-flake", owner="ops" into step-5 aggregation. ClassifyVerdict (C1) runs ONLY here, after exhaustion — NEVER on the first transient failure and NEVER for a non-transient failure.

transient-detection-rules.rule: EXIT CODES (transient): a timeout exit, EHOSTUNREACH, or any connection-failure exit status from the probe.

MESSAGE PATTERNS (transient, case-insensitive substring of the failure output): "Connection refused", "timeout" / "timed out", "service unavailable" / "503", "DNS resolution failed" / "could not resolve host", "ECONNREFUSED", "connection reset".
PROBE MODES (transient): a health-check endpoint reporting not-ready, or a container still starting.
A failure matching NONE of the above is NOT transient: it is a real defect — route it to the leaf-4 code-defect catch-all (C1) and do NOT retry it. Do NOT extend this set with ad-hoc heuristics.

step.action: Short-circuit on the FIRST failing precondition — do not evaluate the rest. (A kind=service probe "fails" only after its transient-retry-loop is exhausted; a successful retry is not a failure.)

check.condition: a precondition failed (env/other on first evaluation; service only after transient-retry-loop exhaustion)
check.action: Call goal-validation-done: goal_id=GOAL_ID, verdict="blocked", findings=[{rule:<spec>, status:"blocked", detail:<remedy>, correction:<remedy>, failure_class:<class>, owner:<owner>}], next_action=<remedy>. If goal-validation-done rejects verdict="blocked" (enum not yet expanded by C1), retry with verdict="fail", keeping the same detail/correction/failure_class/owner on the finding.
Do NOT write signal.json — the daemon is the authoritative emitter of the precondition signal.

step.action: All preconditions satisfied (or none present) — proceed to step 2c.

step.title: C10 re-validation spawn-decision gate
step.action: Decide which active investigators to RE-RUN and which to REUSE by running the read-only CLI:
step.rule: Pass --full when force_full_revalidation is set (the --full input). Pass --final when this is the final cycle before overall pass (all checks expected green). EITHER flag forces every investigator to RERUN for end-to-end regression safety.
The command prints a JSON array of {finding_id, action: "RERUN"|"REUSE", reused_from_cycle, fingerprint}. finding_id matches the investigator name.

If the command errors, prints an empty array, or there is no prior results.json (first cycle / fresh goal), treat EVERY active investigator as RERUN (safe degenerate full run). NEVER block on a missing or corrupt plan.
step.action: Partition the active investigators: action="RERUN" ⇒ spawn an investigator-* worker in step 3; action="REUSE" ⇒ do NOT spawn a worker — carry the prior pass forward as a status="pass" finding tagged with reused_from_cycle and reused_fingerprint (the unchanged fingerprint).
Record the REUSE set for steps 3, 5 and 5b. A reused investigator is never spawned, never times out, and always aggregates as "pass".

step.title: C8 cross-reference investigator artifacts against bindings (spec-defect preflight)
step.action: Reuse C3's binding parse: read the project-root AGENTS.md and docs/architecture/adrs/ to detect binding keywords. If neither exists (or no keyword matches), FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS is empty — skip this step entirely: spawn every active investigator as planned and emit no spec-defect finding. Never treat absent bindings as an error or a block.

Build FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS as the UNION of the forbidden-artifact patterns for every detected binding keyword, using the same binding-forbidden-map the planner uses (task-plan-generate.xml substep 1.45):
step.map: - greenfield → composer.json, vendor/, package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Gemfile.lock, poetry.lock - service-oriented / microservice → shared_db, single shared/monolithic schema.sql, one-DB-for-all-services references - no-orm → doctrine.yaml, *.orm.xml, prisma/schema.prisma, ORM entity-mapping config
step.action: For each ACTIVE investigator (after step 2c partitioning), match each of its referenced files and commands artifacts CONSERVATIVELY (basename / path-suffix) against FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS — a doc that merely mentions the word is NOT a match.

check.condition: an investigator references an artifact in FORBIDDEN_ARTIFACTS
check.action: Short-circuit that investigator: do NOT spawn a worker for it. Record a spec-defect ValidationFinding for step-5 aggregation: {rule:"binding-cross-reference: <artifact> vs <binding>", status:"blocked", detail:"<artifact> contradicts <binding> binding", correction:"regenerate Config without <artifact>", failure_class:"spec-defect", owner:"planner"}.
check.rule: A binding contradiction is ALWAYS failure_class=spec-defect, owner=planner — NEVER a plain pass/fail investigator verdict. owner=planner tops C1's owner-priority order.

step.rule: Investigators with NO forbidden reference are unaffected: they keep their step 2c RERUN/REUSE decision and proceed to step 3 normally. This step removes only contradicting investigators from the spawn set; it never alters the condition-skip logic, the investigator- prefix, or a clean investigator's spawn.

step.title: C1a worker-escalation relay (spec-defect preflight)
step.action: Read GOAL_DIR/escalation.md. If the file does not exist or has escalation_count: 0 / zero entries, SKIP this step entirely — spawn investigators as planned and emit no escalation finding. Absent/empty escalations are never an error or a block.

Parse the entries under "## Escalations" (schema: escalation_count plus one or more "- need: \"...\"" blocks). For EACH entry whose need= is concrete (non-empty, not 'none'/'n/a'/'tbd'/'fix it'), record one ValidationFinding for step-5 aggregation: {rule:"escalation: <need>", status:"blocked", detail:"worker escalated an out-of-scope prerequisite: <need>", correction:"Create prerequisite goal: <need>", failure_class:"spec-defect", owner:"planner"}.
step.rule: The relayed finding MUST be substantive: detail and correction both name the concrete prerequisite. A contentless escalation finding is rejected by HasSubstantiveSpecDefect and would mis-route to ValidationRetries instead of generation — skip any entry with a contentless need= rather than emitting a vague finding.

This preflight does NOT remove or skip any investigator (unlike step 2d's binding short-circuit): the escalation finding is ADDED alongside the normal investigator verdicts. It forces verdict=blocked in step 5 (owner=planner tops the owner-priority order), so the goal cannot roll up to pass while an unconsumed escalation stands. Generation clears/supersedes consumed escalation entries when it creates the prerequisite goal.

step.title: Inline/spawn partition gate — static analysis inline, advanced spawned
step.action: Derive INLINE_SET and SPAWN_SET (each a subset of the step 2c RERUN set; REUSE investigators are in NEITHER set — they carry forward as pass in step 5). PRIMARY source is the read-only CLI; the in-XML predicate is the safe fallback. Run:
step.rule: CYCLE_N is the one-indexed cycle resolved in step 2a. In STANDALONE mode (CYCLE_N unset) pass --cycle 0. Pass --full / --final on the SAME conditions as step 2c so the RERUN set matches exactly.

The command prints {"inline":[finding ids],"spawn":[finding ids],"reason":"..."}: `inline` = the RERUN investigators that run in-window (pure-command / static analysis), `spawn` = the RERUN investigators that need a reasoning worker. WELL-FORMED output (a JSON object carrying BOTH an `inline` array AND a `spawn` array) is AUTHORITATIVE: set INLINE_SET = `inline`, SPAWN_SET = `spawn`, skip the fallback, and go straight to routing. This mirrors taskvisor.InlinePlan (internal/taskvisor/inline.go) — the Go seam owns the split rule; this step only branches on its JSON.

SAFE DEGENERATE: if the command errors, prints nothing, prints unparseable output, OR its JSON lacks BOTH the `inline` and `spawn` keys (e.g. a stale binary still printing the old {"mode","rerun"} shape), DO NOT block — fall back to the IN-XML predicate below to partition the ACTIVE RERUN investigators yourself. The in-XML split is binary-independent and always a safe fallback; never block on a missing/garbled plan.

IN-XML FALLBACK (used ONLY on a degenerate CLI outcome; mirror of taskvisor.IsPureCommand): classify EACH active RERUN investigator. PURE-COMMAND ⇒ INLINE_SET (run in-window, NEVER spawn) — has >=1 Command AND (type == `command` OR (type ∈ {static-analysis, quality-gate, test-execution, architecture-check, environment-check, file-check} AND its Pass text is EXIT-ONLY)). Pass is EXIT-ONLY iff it contains an exit marker ("exit 0", "exit code", "succeeds", "green", "no error", "no violation", "no layer violation", "passes") AND NO semantic marker ("matches expected", "review", "audit", "compliance", "correct", "present", "well-formed", "design"); the semantic veto wins, an empty Pass is NOT exit-only. EVERYTHING ELSE ⇒ SPAWN_SET — reasoning/semantic types (code-review, convention-audit, implementation-check, integration-check), advanced/flaky-external types (e2e-test = Chrome/browser, integration-test), a semantic Pass, an unknown/missing type, or a deterministic type with NO Command. WHEN IN DOUBT, SPAWN.

This is a per-investigator SPLIT, NOT all-or-nothing. Static analysis NEVER spawns an agent. Parallel agents are spawned ONLY for the advanced SPAWN_SET (E2E/Chrome, code-review, debugging, integration). A single advanced investigator no longer drags the static-analysis checks into a fan-out.
step.action: Route on the two sets:

check.condition: INLINE_SET empty AND SPAWN_SET empty (all REUSE — nothing to run)
check.action: Skip steps 3, 3-inline and 4. Go straight to step 4c → step 5; the REUSE set aggregates as pass.

check.condition: SPAWN_SET empty AND INLINE_SET non-empty (pure static-analysis goal)
check.action: NO agents at all. Skip step 3 and step 4 ENTIRELY (zero windows-spawn-worker, zero investigator-* windows). Go to step 3-inline (run the INLINE_SET in-window), then step 4c → step 5.

check.condition: INLINE_SET empty AND SPAWN_SET non-empty (pure advanced goal)
check.action: NO inline checks. Go to step 3 (spawn the SPAWN_SET) → step 4 (collect) → step 4c → step 5 — the legacy fan-out path, byte-identical to before for an all-advanced goal.

check.condition: BOTH non-empty (the SPLIT — static inline + advanced spawned, concurrently)
check.action: FIRST run step 3 to spawn the SPAWN_SET (so the agents investigate concurrently), THEN run step 3-inline to execute the INLINE_SET in THIS window while the agents work, THEN run step 4 to collect the spawned reports, then step 4c → step 5. INLINE_SET inline-findings and SPAWN_SET worker-findings merge into the single step-5 findings array.

step.title: Run the INLINE_SET (static analysis) inline — zero spawns for these checks
step.condition: step 2f routed the INLINE_SET here (SPAWN_SET-empty pure-inline goal, or the BOTH-non-empty split)
step.rule: This branch runs the INLINE_SET ONLY. Issue ZERO windows-spawn-worker calls for INLINE_SET members — every INLINE_SET finding comes from an in-window command run, never a worker. The SPAWN_SET (advanced investigators) is spawned SEPARATELY in step 3 and runs concurrently; do NOT re-run or collect it here.

Operate on the INLINE_SET ONLY (the pure-command subset of the step 2c RERUN set, per the step 2f partition). REUSE investigators are NOT executed here; they aggregate as pass in step 5 with reused_from_cycle/reused_fingerprint exactly as on the spawned path.
step.action: For EACH INLINE_SET investigator, in order, do the following IN THIS WINDOW:
step.substep: Validate each command against the blocklist (rm, mv, cp, tee, chmod, chown, touch, sed -i, kill, mkfs, dd, >, >>) BEFORE running. Skip any destructive command with finding "command skipped: destructive operation forbidden".

Run each safe command via Bash with a per-command timeout (wrap as `timeout <T>s <command>`). Bound T so the SUM of inline command timeouts stays under the daemon validate_timeout envelope — use the same 10-minute ceiling a single worker had: divide the per-worker 600s budget across the INLINE_SET commands (e.g. ceil(600 / max(1, command_count)) s each), so a single hung command cannot blow the daemon budget.

On a command failure that is a transient-prone probe, re-run it through step 2b's <transient-retry-loop> (same transient-detection-rules). A deterministic non-zero exit / assertion failure is NOT transient — do not retry it. Pass on first success.

Classify the result using investigate-worker.xml's <classification> block VERBATIM: GREEN OBJECTIVE CHECK ⇒ status=pass; env/connectivity signature ⇒ blocked/env-config|infra-flake/ops; assertion-on-code-output ⇒ fail/code-defect/implementer; ambiguous/untestable ⇒ error/validator-error/ops; leaf-4 catch-all ⇒ a non-pass finding with no clear class is fail/code-defect/implementer. failure_class and owner are REQUIRED on every non-pass finding.

Build EXACTLY ONE ValidationFinding for this investigator (rule = investigator name, status, detail, correction-if-non-pass, failing_command + output_excerpt on failure, plus failure_class/owner on non-pass, plus `validation_mode: "inline"` — REQUIRED on every inline-built finding; the B7 inline marker mirroring reused_from_cycle) and add it to the step-5 findings array.
step.action: OPTIONAL audit: write the raw command/output excerpts to <CYCLE_DIR>/inline-evidence.md (audit only — namespaced under the C7 CYCLE_DIR, never a flat or prior-cycle path). This file is evidence, not a verdict source; goal-validation-done is still the single report channel.

Surface the B7 instrumentation signal: emit a greppable line `inv_inlined=<N> inv_spawned=<M>` where N == the INLINE_SET count just executed inline and M == the SPAWN_SET count spawned in step 3 this cycle (M=0 on a pure-inline goal). This is what makes the saving measurable. The daemon's COUNTERS line derives inv_inlined from the `validation_mode: "inline"` tags on the findings, keeping the log and this evidence line consistent.
step.rule: PRESERVE the other preflight findings: a C8 spec-defect (2d) and each C1a escalation (2e) finding still aggregate alongside the inline findings in step 5 — inline execution does NOT touch them, so an inline cycle can still roll up to verdict "blocked". C10 reuse (2c) and the C7 cycle-dir guard are untouched.
step.action: If a SPAWN_SET was spawned in step 3 this cycle (the BOTH-non-empty split), proceed to step 4 to collect the spawned reports, then step 4c → step 5. Otherwise (pure-inline goal, zero spawns) proceed directly to step 4c (convergence guard) → step 5 (aggregate). Do NOT windows-kill anything for the INLINE_SET (no windows were created for inline checks); step 4 kills the spawned SPAWN_SET workers.

step.title: Spawn investigator-* workers (SPAWN_SET — advanced investigators only)
step.rule: SPLIT: spawn a worker ONLY for SPAWN_SET investigators — the advanced/reasoning checks the step 2f partition selected (code-review, e2e-test = Chrome/browser, integration-test, semantic-Pass, unknown/missing type, or a deterministic type with no command). INLINE_SET (static-analysis / pure-command) investigators are NEVER spawned — they run in-window via step 3-inline. Worker count == SPAWN_SET count.

C8: an investigator short-circuited to a spec-defect in step 2d is NOT spawned (its blocked finding is already recorded for step-5 aggregation). Worker count excludes REUSE, INLINE_SET, and spec-defect investigators.
C10: a SPAWN_SET investigator is by construction in the step 2c RERUN set. REUSE investigators are NOT spawned — they carry forward their prior-cycle pass.
step.action: For each SPAWN_SET investigator, call windows-spawn-worker with:
step.params: - supervisorWid: VALIDATOR_WID (i.e. "validator") - subtask: "Investigate: <investigator name>" - contextFile: path to goal.md ($ARGUMENTS) - scope: investigator definition (type, paths, commands, check, pass/fail criteria, retry config if present) - context: goal description + acceptance criteria from goal.md - deliverable: custom investigation report format (see <worker-deliverable>) - prefix: "investigator-" - workingDirectory: WORKTREE_DIR if set (step 2a), else OMIT (empty). E1-1c: this starts the investigator-* worker's shell in the goal's git worktree (tmux -c) so its SCOPE commands observe ONLY this goal's edits. When WORKTREE_DIR is unset, omit the field — the worker keeps the session-default cwd, byte-identical to before.
step.rule: C7: inject the resolved report dir into EACH worker's task message — append "CYCLE_DIR=<CYCLE_DIR> CYCLE_N=<CYCLE_N> GOAL_ID=<GOAL_ID>" to the context AND state in scope: "Write your report to <CYCLE_DIR>/investigator-<N>-<slug>.md". The worker writes ONLY within CYCLE_DIR — never a flat or prior-cycle path.

E1-1c: when WORKTREE_DIR is set (step 2a), ALSO append "WORKTREE_DIR=<WORKTREE_DIR>" to the context so the worker can resolve relative SCOPE paths and run SCOPE commands against the worktree (it is also -c'd there via workingDirectory). The CYCLE_DIR report path is UNCHANGED — reports still land under the base-shared .tmux-cli, never inside the worktree's working set. When WORKTREE_DIR is unset, append nothing.
step.action: Record from the response: workerName (investigator-N), window info. Update tracking table: status=spawned, spawn_time=now.
step.critical: ALWAYS use windows-spawn-worker with prefix: "investigator-" — never assemble manually via windows-create + windows-send.

step.title: Idle and collect reports (with timeout)
step.action: Do NOTHING. Wait for windows-message from workers.
step.rule: NEVER use sleep. NEVER poll windows-list while idle.

Stop-hook idle notifications ("Idling — awaiting supervisor task payload" / "Report delivered to supervisor. Idling for accept or pushback") are expected /tmux:execute lifecycle output. Do not REMIND, do not advance state — wait for the next real message.

branch.action: C7 path guard: accept the report ONLY if its path is within the current CYCLE_DIR. Clean both paths (resolve "." and "..") and require the report path to have CYCLE_DIR as a path prefix (equivalently, the relative path from CYCLE_DIR to the report contains no leading "../"). A path OUTSIDE CYCLE_DIR — e.g. a prior cycle's research/cycle-<N-1>/ or a flat research/{y-m-d-hh}/ — is treated as a MISSING report: mark this investigator status=error (failure_class=validator-error, owner=ops, detail "report path outside current cycle dir — possible stale prior-cycle report"), kill the worker via windows-kill, and do NOT draw a verdict from it. Continue waiting on the others.
Read the report file at the cited path.
Extract VERDICT (pass|fail|blocked|error) and FINDINGS (including failure_class/owner for non-pass checks) from the report.
Update tracking table: status=completed, report_path=file path.
Kill the worker window via windows-kill with the NAME (e.g. "investigator-1").
Check if all workers have reached terminal status. If not, continue waiting.
Attempt to answer from goal.md context (acceptance criteria, description, investigation config).
If answerable: reply via windows-message to that INV_WID with the answer.
If unanswerable: reply via windows-message with [SUPERVISOR:NARROW-SCOPE <reduced problem statement that the worker CAN answer without the missing info>].
Update tracking table: status=failed, store the failure reason.
Kill the worker window via windows-kill with the NAME.
This investigator is treated as an ERROR finding (failure_class=validator-error, owner=ops) in aggregation — the validator could not obtain its verdict.
Check if all workers have reached terminal status. If not, continue waiting.

branch.tag: worker emitted content without [EXECUTE:...] header AND not a stop-hook idle line
branch.action: Reply ONCE via windows-message: [SUPERVISOR:REMIND] Respond only with [EXECUTE:DONE file=<abs-path>] or [EXECUTE:NEED_INPUT] or [EXECUTE:FAILED reason=...].
Do not advance state for that worker.

timeout.rule: Track wall-clock time since each worker was spawned. If a worker has not reported within 10 minutes of its spawn time, it has timed out.
timeout.action: Send [SUPERVISOR:KILL] via windows-message to the timed-out worker.
Wait briefly, then kill via windows-kill with the NAME.
This investigator is treated as an ERROR finding (failure_class=validator-error, owner=ops) with detail "investigation timeout — worker did not report within 10 minutes".

step.action: Continue routing messages until ALL spawned workers have a terminal status (completed, failed, or timeout).

step.title: Cross-cycle repeat escalation (convergence guard)
step.action: List GOAL_DIR/corrections/ and find the highest-numbered existing cycle-N.md (the PRIOR cycle's corrections — the current cycle has not written yet). If none exists (this is the first failing cycle), SKIP this step: every finding is first-occurrence.
Read that prior cycle-N.md and extract PRIOR_FAILS = the set of investigator names under its "## Failing Investigators" section (the "### <name>" headers).

For each finding failing THIS cycle with failure_class=code-defect (owner=implementer): if its investigator name is in PRIOR_FAILS, it is a REPEATED finding that an implementer cycle did not converge. Reclassify it IN PLACE before step 5: set failure_class="spec-defect", owner="planner", and PREPEND to its correction: "REPEATED across cycles — the implementer correction did not converge. Planner: make this criterion objectively buildable per GM-09b/GM-09c — decompose the acceptance item, add the exact presence-grep to the Investigation Config, and name the deliverable file(s) and the throwing exception so the next implementer cycle has a falsifiable target."
step.rule: Escalate ONLY code-defect/implementer findings. Leave env-config/infra-flake (owner=ops), validator-error (owner=ops), and findings already spec-defect/planner unchanged. A finding NOT in PRIOR_FAILS is first-occurrence — leave it code-defect so the implementer gets its first (now objective) correction before any escalation.
Match by investigator NAME (the stable "Investigator N: <name>" label). Do not escalate on a different investigator that merely shares a failure_class.

This step only mutates failure_class/owner/correction of already-failing findings; it never adds, removes, or passes a finding. The step-5 roll-up then sees the escalated owner and rolls the verdict to "blocked" (spec-defect outranks nothing that is still code-defect, but with the repeat now spec-defect the daemon re-plans instead of re-dispatching the implementer on the unchanged finding).

step.title: Aggregate findings
step.rule: C8: spec-defect investigators (step 2d) contribute a status="blocked", failure_class="spec-defect", owner="planner" ValidationFinding WITHOUT having spawned a worker. They force the overall verdict to "blocked" — a binding contradiction can never roll up to pass.

C1a: each concrete worker-escalation (step 2e) contributes a status="blocked", failure_class="spec-defect", owner="planner" ValidationFinding (rule "escalation: <need>", correction "Create prerequisite goal: <need>") WITHOUT having spawned a worker. It forces the overall verdict to "blocked" — an unconsumed out-of-scope prerequisite can never roll up to pass.

C10: REUSE investigators (step 2c) contribute a status="pass" ValidationFinding WITHOUT having spawned a worker — carry reused_from_cycle and reused_fingerprint on the finding. They never affect the verdict adversely (a reused pass is still a pass).

C4-cont: a transient-retry exhaustion (step 2b <transient-retry-loop>) contributes a status="blocked", failure_class="infra-flake", owner="ops" ValidationFinding WITHOUT a worker verdict. It rolls up to verdict "blocked"; ClassifyVerdict's owner-priority (planner>ops) leaves owner=ops UNCHANGED unless a co-occurring spec-defect (planner) finding outranks it. The emitter's owner is never overwritten.
step.action: Merge the INLINE_SET inline findings (step 3-inline, each tagged validation_mode "inline"), all SPAWN_SET worker results (step 4), AND the reuse set into a single findings array (one ValidationFinding per investigator):
step.format: For each investigator: - rule: investigator name (e.g. "Investigator 1: DDD compliance") - status: "pass", "fail", "blocked", or "error" (from worker's VERDICT; use "error" for failed/timed-out workers — the validator could not obtain a verdict) - detail: summary of what was checked and the result (from worker's FINDINGS, or failure/timeout reason) - correction: if failed, what needs to change (from worker's FINDINGS); empty if passed - failure_class: REQUIRED for every non-pass finding — carry the worker's failure_class (code-defect|env-config|infra-flake|spec-defect|validator-error). Failed/timed-out workers → validator-error. Leaf-4 catch-all: a non-pass finding with no clear class → code-defect. Never drop this field. - owner: REQUIRED for every non-pass finding — carry the worker's owner (implementer|ops|planner). Failed/timed-out workers → ops. Never drop this field.
step.action: Determine overall verdict by roll-up precedence (highest first):
step.rule: If ANY finding is a code defect (status fail + failure_class code-defect, or the leaf-4 catch-all) → verdict "fail".
Else if ANY finding is "blocked" → verdict "blocked".
Else if ANY finding is "error" (failed/timed-out worker) → verdict "error".
Else (all investigators reported "pass") → verdict "pass".

check.condition: verdict is fail, blocked, or error

step.title: All PASS — report success
step.action: Call goal-validation-done MCP with:
step.params: - goal_id: GOAL_ID - verdict: "pass" - findings: array of ValidationFinding (one per active investigator, all status="pass"; reused investigators carry reused_from_cycle + reused_fingerprint; inline-executed investigators carry validation_mode "inline") - next_action: "" - results: array of {id: investigator name, status: "pass", scope_files: the investigator's Paths, changed_files: files changed this cycle} — one per active investigator (RERUN and REUSE alike). The server computes each finding's input fingerprint and writes the orchestrator-owned results.json ledger for the NEXT cycle's reuse gate.
step.action: Kill any remaining investigator-* windows via windows-kill (should already be killed in step 4, but ensure cleanup).
Idle — daemon reads signal.json and manages lifecycle.

step.title: Any FAIL — write corrections and report failure
step.action: Determine the next cycle number:
step.substep: Scan GOAL_DIR/corrections/ for existing cycle-N.md files.
Find the highest N among existing files. Set next_N = highest_N + 1.
If no cycle files exist, set next_N = 1.
Create GOAL_DIR/corrections/ directory if it does not exist.
step.action: Write GOAL_DIR/corrections/cycle-<next_N>.md with the following structure:
step.template:
# Investigation Corrections — Cycle <next_N>
Goal: <GOAL_ID> Timestamp: <ISO 8601> Attempt: <next_N> Verdict: FAIL
## Summary <1-2 sentences: what was investigated, how many passed, how many failed>
## Failing Investigators
### <Investigator Name> **What was checked:** <brief description from investigator config> **What went wrong:** <specific finding with file:line references where applicable> **Failing command(s):** ``` <exact command and output excerpt> ``` **Expected state:** <what "fixed" looks like — the pass criteria from the investigator config> **Correction:** <actionable fix description>
(repeat for each failing investigator)
## Passing Investigators <list names of investigators that passed THIS cycle (freshly re-run), one per line>
## Reused Findings <C10: list each REUSE investigator (step 2c) on its own line, prefixed with its reuse marker, e.g. "[REUSED FROM CYCLE <N>] <investigator name> (fingerprint <reused_fingerprint>)". Omit this section entirely if there were no reused findings.>
step.rule: C10: the `[REUSED FROM CYCLE <N>]` marker is authored HERE into cycle-N.md by this orchestrator; the daemon's writeDispatchMd concatenates corrections/cycle-N.md verbatim, so the marker flows into dispatch.md unchanged. Do not expect the daemon to add it.
Each failing investigator's section MUST be summarized to max ~500 tokens to prevent context window exhaustion when the daemon re-dispatches with corrections.
Include exact failing commands and their output — the executor needs these to verify its fix.
Include expected state (pass criteria) — the executor needs to know what success looks like.
step.action: Call goal-validation-done MCP with:
step.params: - goal_id: GOAL_ID - verdict: the aggregated verdict from step 5 — "fail", "blocked", or "error" (NOT hardcoded; the daemon routes by it: fail/blocked re-dispatch the implementer/owner, error re-runs validation only) - findings: array of ValidationFinding (one per active investigator, with status, detail, correction, and — for every non-pass finding — failure_class and owner; reused investigators carry reused_from_cycle + reused_fingerprint; inline-executed investigators carry validation_mode "inline") - next_action: "re-dispatch with corrections" - results: array of {id: investigator name, status, scope_files: the investigator's Paths, changed_files: files changed this cycle} — one per active investigator (RERUN and REUSE alike), so the server refreshes the orchestrator-owned results.json ledger for the next cycle's reuse gate.
step.action: Kill any remaining investigator-* windows via windows-kill.
Idle — daemon reads signal.json, checks retry limits, and re-dispatches if allowed.

worker-deliverable.note: Custom deliverable format passed to windows-spawn-worker via the deliverable parameter. This REPLACES the standard FINDINGS/RISKS/RECOMMENDATION/FILES format. Investigation workers produce reports with these sections.
worker-deliverable.template: DELIVERABLE (write these sections INSIDE the report .md; do NOT paste them into tmux): - ## VERDICT: exactly one word — "pass", "fail", "blocked", or "error" - ## FINDINGS: What was checked and what was found. Include: - Each check performed (command run, file inspected, etc.) - Result of each check (output excerpt, pass/fail) - For failures: file:line references where applicable - For failures: exact command output (relevant excerpt) - For failures: what the expected state should be (pass criteria) - ## EVIDENCE: Raw command output excerpts proving the verdict. Keep excerpts focused — include only the relevant lines, not full command output. Max 500 tokens for this section.

execution-rules.rule: windows-spawn-worker: single atomic call per worker, with prefix: "investigator-"
NEVER assemble spawn steps manually — always use windows-spawn-worker
windows-message: all outbound communication to workers (answers, narrow-scope, reminders, kill)
windows-kill: only on completed/failed/timed-out workers; never on VALIDATOR_WID; pass window NAME (e.g. "investigator-1")
goal-validation-done: called exactly ONCE at the end with the aggregated verdict
No pushback cycle — workers report pass or fail, no revision loop
Always Read the file cited in a DONE before extracting verdict; never rely on inline body
If a worker sends DONE without file=, REMIND once; do not accept inline-body DONE
Corrections file is the ONLY file investigate.xml writes (besides goal-validation-done signal)
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

llm.critical-rules: * You run inside the "validator" window — the daemon owns your lifecycle * NEVER kill VALIDATOR_WID — idle after goal-validation-done, daemon kills you * NEVER use sleep — idle is the trigger for incoming messages * NEVER use windows-send — use windows-message for all worker communication * NEVER accept partial results — ALL spawned workers must reach terminal state before aggregation * NEVER use pushback — investigation has no revision loop * NEVER modify project files — corrections/cycle-N.md and goal-validation-done are the only writes * ALWAYS spawn workers with prefix: "investigator-" * ALWAYS call goal-validation-done exactly ONCE * ALWAYS kill investigator-* workers after collecting their reports (or on timeout/failure) * Pass window NAME to windows-kill (e.g. "investigator-1"), not @id * Each investigator section in corrections/cycle-N.md max ~500 tokens * Timeout: 10 minutes per worker — kill and treat as FAIL
